task: Observe flow of materials and listen for machine malfunctions, such as jamming or spillage, and notify supervisors if corrective actions fail.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical presence at work site to observe material flow and listen for machine malfunctions in real-time.

task: Lay out and fabricate metal structural parts such as plates, bulkheads, and frames.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of metal materials, operation of fabrication tools/machinery, and hands-on construction of structural components.

task: Set up, operate, or tend plating or coating machines to coat metal or plastic products with chromium, zinc, copper, cadmium, nickel, or other metal to protect or decorate surfaces.
rationale: Setting up, operating, and tending plating/coating machines requires hands-on operation of physical equipment and machinery to execute the coating process.

task: Monitor machine operations to determine whether adjustments are necessary, stopping machines when problems occur.
rationale: Requires physical operation of machinery—monitoring equipment, making mechanical adjustments, and operating shutdown controls.

task: Operate and maintain laser plate-making equipment that converts electronic data to plates without the use of film.
rationale: Involves operating and maintaining physical laser equipment—direct hands-on manipulation and operation of machinery.

task: Inspect track for defects such as broken rails and switch malfunctions.
rationale: Physical inspection of rails and switches requires being on-site, examining and touching track infrastructure for visible and tactile defects.

task: Test and charge batteries.
rationale: Physical manipulation and operation of batteries and charging equipment required.

task: Report any unusual circumstances concerning mail delivery, including the condition of street letter boxes.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of letter boxes and street conditions; involves observing and assessing tangible objects on-site.

task: Provide assistance to workers engaged in construction or extraction activities, using hand tools or other equipment.
rationale: Assisting with construction/extraction using hand tools requires direct physical manipulation and operation of equipment at work sites.

task: Assemble and cement sections of stained glass together.
rationale: Assembling and cementing glass sections requires physical manipulation of tangible objects and hands-on craftsmanship.

task: Remove fixtures such as pictures, door knobs, lamps, or electric switch covers prior to painting.
rationale: Removing fixtures requires physical manipulation of tangible objects like pictures, doorknobs, and lamps.

task: Remove asbestos or lead from surfaces, using hand or power tools such as scrapers, vacuums, or high-pressure sprayers.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical operation of hand/power tools (scrapers, vacuums, sprayers) and manipulation of contaminated surfaces.

task: Assemble and install communication equipment such as data and telephone communication lines, wiring, switching equipment, wiring frames, power apparatus, computer systems, and networks.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of equipment, wiring, and systems installation—hands-on assembly and operational work.

task: Clean switches and replace contact points, using vacuum hoses, solvents, and hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of equipment, use of hand tools, solvents, and vacuum hoses to clean and repair switches—hands-on mechanical work.

task: Test battery charges, and replace or recharge batteries as necessary.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of batteries—testing, replacing, and recharging them involves tangible object handling.

task: Lubricate wearing surfaces of mechanical parts, using oils or other lubricants.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of mechanical parts and application of lubricants using tools or hands.

task: Trace and tag meters or house lines.
rationale: Tracing and tagging meters requires physical presence on-site to locate, inspect, mark, and label utility lines and meters in the field.

task: Vary air pressure flowing into regulators and turn handles to assess functioning of valves and pistons.
rationale: Physically manipulating equipment (turning handles) and operating machinery (varying air pressure in regulators) to test valve and piston functionality.

task: Cut, strip, and bend wire leads at ends of coils, using pliers and wire scrapers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of wire using hand tools (pliers, scrapers) to cut, strip, and bend materials.

task: Tend machines that bond cope and drag together to form completed shell molds.
rationale: Operating machines that physically bond shell mold components together requires hands-on machinery operation and physical oversight at the work site.

task: Select lumber to be used for patterns.
rationale: Selecting lumber involves physically examining and manipulating wood materials to choose appropriate pieces for use in patterns.

task: Tighten pulleys or add abrasives to maintain cutting speeds.
rationale: Tightening pulleys and adding abrasives require physical manipulation of equipment parts to maintain machinery.

task: Grade, classify, or sort products according to sizes, weights, colors, or other specifications.
rationale: Grading and sorting products requires physical handling, inspection, and manipulation of tangible items based on visible specifications.

task: Clean semiconductor wafers using cleaning equipment, such as chemical baths, automatic wafer cleaners, or blow-off wands.
rationale: Requires physical operation of cleaning equipment and manipulation of wafers, chemical handling, and hands-on equipment operation.

task: Place products in containers according to grade and mark grades on containers.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of products, placing them into containers, and physically marking containers—hands-on work.

task: Fit and fasten wallboard or drywall into position on wood or metal frameworks, using glue, nails, or screws.
rationale: Physical manipulation of materials (wallboard), use of tools (nails, screws, glue application), and hands-on assembly work.

task: Move or position materials such as marble slabs, using cranes, hoists, or dollies.
rationale: Operating cranes, hoists, and dollies to move and position marble slabs is physical equipment operation requiring hands-on machinery use.

task: Observe leveling indicator arms to verify levelness and alignment of tracks.
rationale: Physical inspection requiring being on-site to observe and verify leveling indicators and track alignment.

task: Perform pumping tests to assess well performance.
rationale: Pumping tests require operation of pumping equipment and hands-on measurement of well performance on-site.

task: Clean and prepare sites for excavation or boring.
rationale: Requires physical site preparation, debris removal, marking, and equipment setup at the work location.

task: Test and replace electrical components and wiring, using test meters, soldering equipment, and hand tools.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of electrical components, operation of soldering equipment and hand tools, and hands-on testing/replacement work.

task: Observe and test operation of appliances following installation, and make any initial installation adjustments that are necessary.
rationale: Requires physical operation of appliances, hands-on testing, and direct adjustment of equipment after installation.

task: Inspect and test docks, ships, buoyage systems, plant intakes or outflows, or underwater pipelines, cables, or sewers, using closed circuit television, still photography, and testing equipment.
rationale: Requires operating specialized equipment (closed circuit television, photography gear, testing devices) and physical inspection at work sites to examine underwater/dock infrastructure.

task: Cut and weld steel, using underwater welding equipment, jigs, and supports.
rationale: Primary execution requires operating welding equipment, physically manipulating steel, and using tools and jigs underwater—all manual, hands-on work.

task: Mask off mold areas not to be laminated, using cellophane, wax paper, masking tape, or special sprays containing mold-release substances.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of masking materials and application of sprays to mold surfaces using tangible objects and tools.

task: Unwind lengths of yarn, thread, or twine from spools and wind onto bobbins.
rationale: Physical manipulation of yarn/thread and operation of winding equipment requires hands-on work with tangible materials.

task: Fit, install, and secure material on frames, using hand tools, power tools, glue, cement, or staples.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials and operation of hand/power tools to fit, install, and secure items on frames.

task: Perform tests to check the qualities and grades of products, such as assessing levels of bottom sediment, water, and foreign materials in oil samples, using centrifugal testers.
rationale: Performing tests on oil samples using centrifugal testers requires operating physical machinery and handling tangible samples to assess product quality.

task: Count or weigh and bundle items.
rationale: Counting, weighing, and bundling items requires physical manipulation and handling of tangible objects.

task: Mark cutting lines around patterns or templates, or follow layout points, using squares, rules, and straightedges, and chalk, pencils, or scribes.
rationale: Involves physical manipulation of tools (squares, rules, straightedges, chalk, pencils, scribes) and marking material surfaces—requires hands-on execution.

task: Unroll, lay out, attach, or mount materials or items on cutting tables or machines.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of materials—unrolling, laying out, and mounting items on machines. Direct hands-on execution.

task: Perform general labor duties such as repairing booms.
rationale: Repairing booms requires physical manipulation of equipment and tools to fix tangible machinery components.

task: Operate power-driven pumps that transfer liquids, semi-liquids, gases, or powdered materials.
rationale: Operating power-driven pumps involves physical equipment operation and control to transfer materials, requiring hands-on manipulation and monitoring of machinery.

task: Assemble or adjust parts or related electrical units of prototypes to prepare for testing.
rationale: Assembling and adjusting physical parts requires hands-on manipulation of electrical components and mechanical objects.

task: Provide adapted physical education services to students with intellectual disabilities, autism, traumatic brain injury, orthopedic impairments, or other disabling condition.
rationale: Providing adapted PE services requires hands-on physical demonstration, movement instruction, and bodily presence to guide, support, and instruct students through physical activities and exercises.

task: Diagnose infections, such as Hepatitis B and Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS), by conducting tests to detect the antibodies that patients' immune systems make to fight such infections.
rationale: Conducting diagnostic tests involves physical manipulation of lab equipment, specimens, and samples to detect antibodies—hands-on laboratory work.

task: Assess birthing environments to ensure cleanliness, safety, and the availability of appropriate supplies.
rationale: Assessing birthing environments requires physical presence and inspection of tangible conditions, supplies, and equipment on-site.

task: Prepare or serve hot or cold beverages, such as coffee, espresso drinks, blended coffees, or teas.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of equipment, ingredients, and containers to prepare and serve beverages.

task: Install active solar systems, including solar collectors, concentrators, pumps, or fans.
rationale: Requires physical installation of equipment components including collectors, pumps, and fans on site.

task: Apply weather seal, such as pipe flashings and sealants, to roof penetrations and structural devices.
rationale: Applying sealants and flashings requires physical manipulation of materials and tools on the roof to seal penetrations.

task: Operate equipment to start, stop, or regulate biomass-fueled generators, generator units, boilers, engines, or auxiliary systems.
rationale: Operating equipment (generators, boilers, engines) requires hands-on manipulation of controls and physical machinery operation at the work site.

task: Develop laser-processed designs, such as laser-cut medical devices.
rationale: Operating laser equipment to process and cut materials into designs requires hands-on operation of physical machinery and tools.

task: Set up, operate, or monitor invasive equipment and devices, such as colostomy or tracheotomy equipment, mechanical ventilators, catheters, gastrointestinal tubes, and central lines.
rationale: Direct physical manipulation and operation of medical devices and equipment on patients requires hands-on clinical execution.

task: Obtain specimens or samples for laboratory work.
rationale: Obtaining specimens/samples requires physical collection, handling, and manipulation of tangible materials for lab work.

task: Transport specimens, laboratory items, or pharmacy items, ensuring proper documentation and delivery to authorized personnel.
rationale: Transporting physical items requires physical movement and handling of tangible objects to authorized locations.

task: Train other medical personnel in phlebotomy or laboratory techniques.
rationale: Training involves demonstrating hands-on phlebotomy and lab techniques physically to students, not just verbal instruction.

task: Inspect completed work to ensure all hardware is tight, antennas are level, hangers are properly fastened, proper support is in place, or adequate weather proofing has been installed.
rationale: Physical inspection requires being on-site, examining hardware, antennas, and hangers to verify tightness, levelness, and fastening—requires hands-on assessment and tangible object interaction.

task: Perform regular maintenance of laboratory equipment by inspecting, calibrating, cleaning, or sterilizing.
rationale: Task requires hands-on physical manipulation and inspection of laboratory equipment—inspecting, calibrating, cleaning, sterilizing are all physical maintenance activities.

task: Measure or weigh ingredients used in laboratory testing.
rationale: Measuring and weighing ingredients requires physical manipulation of laboratory equipment (scales, measuring devices) and handling of tangible materials.

task: Assist mechanics and perform various mechanical duties, such as changing oil or checking and replacing batteries.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of vehicle components: draining/changing oil, removing/replacing batteries. Hands-on mechanical work.

task: Assist in bus loading and unloading.
rationale: Loading and unloading involves physical handling and movement of luggage/cargo and bodies on/off a bus.

task: Sweep or vacuum the cremation chamber to retrieve remains for storage in an urn or other container.
rationale: Physical manipulation and collection of cremated remains using tools (sweeping/vacuuming) and transferring to containers.

task: Measure and mark cutting lines on materials, using a ruler, pencil, chalk, and marking gauge.
rationale: Requires physical use of tools (ruler, pencil, chalk, gauge) and manual marking of tangible materials.

task: Verify trueness of structure, using plumb bob and level.
rationale: Using plumb bob and level requires physical manipulation of measuring tools and hands-on inspection of structures.

task: Guide and stop cars by switching, applying brakes, or placing scotches, or wooden wedges, between wheels and rails.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of objects (scotches, wedges), operation of brakes, and manual positioning of tangible items to control cars.

task: Connect and turn regulator valves to activate and adjust gas flow and pressure so that desired flames are obtained.
rationale: Physically connecting and turning regulator valves requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects and equipment to control gas flow and pressure.

task: Inspect locomotives to detect damaged or worn parts.
rationale: Inspecting locomotives requires physical presence, hands-on examination, and touching/visual inspection of parts for damage and wear.

task: Examine patients to diagnose oral health conditions and diseases.
rationale: Physical examination of patients' mouths requires hands-on inspection, manipulation, and direct sensory assessment of oral structures.

task: Count money in cash drawers at the beginning of shifts to ensure that amounts are correct and that there is adequate change.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of cash and coins, counting and handling tangible currency objects in drawers.

task: Prime and paint repaired surfaces, using paint sprayguns and motorized sanders.
rationale: Requires operation of physical tools (paint sprayers, motorized sanders) and manual manipulation of surfaces for priming and painting.

task: Fit and secure windows, vinyl roofs, and metal trim to vehicle bodies, using caulking guns, adhesive brushes, and mallets.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation of tools (caulking guns, adhesive brushes, mallets) and materials (windows, vinyl roofs, trim) on vehicle bodies.

task: Fabricate or repair counters, benches, partitions, or other wooden structures, such as sheds or outbuildings.
rationale: Primary execution requires physical manipulation of wood and tools to fabricate, assemble, or repair structures. Hands-on construction work.

task: Load and unload baggage in baggage compartments.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation—lifting, carrying, and placing baggage into/from compartments.

task: Test and measure patient's strength, motor development and function, sensory perception, functional capacity, or respiratory or circulatory efficiency and record data.
rationale: Physical examination and hands-on testing of patient strength, motor function, and sensory perception requires direct physical interaction with the patient.

task: Operate zoom lenses, changing images according to specifications and rehearsal instructions.
rationale: Operating zoom lenses involves physical manipulation of camera equipment and tools to adjust imaging based on specifications and instructions.

task: Examine patients to obtain information about their physical condition.
rationale: Examining patients requires physical interaction—palpation, vital sign measurement, and hands-on assessment.

task: Conduct electrocardiogram (EKG), phonocardiogram, echocardiogram, stress testing, or other cardiovascular tests to record patients' cardiac activity, using specialized electronic test equipment, recording devices, or laboratory instruments.
rationale: Performing cardiovascular tests requires operating specialized medical equipment, attaching devices to patients, and hands-on technical execution to record cardiac activity.

task: Perform a variety of duties as requested by client, such as obtaining household supplies or running errands.
rationale: Obtaining household supplies and running errands requires physical movement, shopping, and handling/carrying goods.

task: Transport patients to and from treatment areas, using wheelchairs or providing standing support.
rationale: Task requires physical manipulation and bodily movement to operate wheelchairs and provide hands-on support for patient mobility.

task: Clean and drive funeral vehicles, such as cars or hearses, in funeral processions.
rationale: Cleaning vehicles involves physical manipulation and maintenance work; driving requires operation of a motor vehicle.

task: Bolt component parts together to form tower assemblies, using hand tools.
rationale: Task involves physical manipulation of components using hand tools to assemble tower structures.

task: Fill processing or cooking containers, such as kettles, rotating cookers, pressure cookers, or vats, with ingredients, by opening valves, by starting pumps or injectors, or by hand.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of containers and equipment (opening valves, starting pumps, manual filling).

task: Drive vehicles to or from workshops or customers' workplaces or homes.
rationale: Operating a vehicle requires physical manipulation of controls and active bodily engagement with machinery and the road.

task: Maintain or repair agricultural facilities, equipment, or tools to ensure operational readiness, safety, and cleanliness.
rationale: Maintaining and repairing facilities, equipment, and tools requires hands-on physical manipulation and operation of tools to ensure functionality.

task: Cut, trim and shape hair or hairpieces, based on customers' instructions, hair type, and facial features, using clippers, scissors, trimmers and razors.
rationale: Requires physical manipulation of tools (clippers, scissors, razors) and direct hands-on cutting/shaping of hair.

task: Operate cash registers to receive payments from patrons.
rationale: Operating a cash register requires physical manipulation of objects (cash, cards, buttons) and interaction with a physical device.

task: Provide a variety of materials and resources for children to explore, manipulate, and use, both in learning activities and in imaginative play.
rationale: Providing and arranging physical materials requires hands-on manipulation of tangible objects for children's learning and play activities.

task: Perform fire protection or suppression duties, such as constructing fire breaks or disposing of brush.
rationale: Constructing fire breaks and disposing of brush requires physical labor, tool use, and direct manipulation of materials and terrain.

task: Monitor functioning of equipment and make necessary modifications to ensure system operates in conformance with specifications.
rationale: Monitoring and modifying equipment requires physical inspection, hands-on adjustment, and operation of machinery to ensure conformance with specifications.

task: Design and fabricate dental prostheses, or supervise dental technicians and laboratory bench workers who construct the devices.
rationale: Design AND fabricate requires physical manipulation of materials/tools. Supervision alone would be text-based, but the task includes actual fabrication work.

task: Use complementary aids, such as infrared lamps, wet compresses, ice, and whirlpool baths to promote clients' recovery, relaxation, and well-being.
rationale: Applying physical therapeutic aids (lamps, compresses, ice, whirlpool baths) requires direct hands-on manipulation and bodily presence to deliver treatment to clients.

task: Investigate crop, property, or habitat damage or destruction or instances of water pollution to determine causes and to advise property owners of preventive measures.
rationale: Requires physical on-site investigation of damage, inspecting objects, taking samples, and direct observation of environmental conditions.

task: Perform tie spacing layout and measure, mark, drill or cut.
rationale: Involves physical measurement, marking, drilling, and cutting—hands-on work with tangible materials and tools.

task: Check for and remove moisture or contamination in damaged areas and keep areas dry until repairs are complete.
rationale: Requires physical inspection of damaged areas, removing moisture/contamination, and maintaining dry conditions—hands-on environmental management.

task: Cut and peel lead sheathing and insulation from defective or newly installed cables and conduits prior to splicing.
rationale: Cutting and peeling lead sheathing requires physical manipulation of cables and tools to remove material from tangible objects.

task: Clean and polish furniture and fixtures.
rationale: Physical manipulation of objects using cleaning and polishing materials and tools to clean and polish tangible furniture and fixtures.

task: Replenish supplies, such as drinking glasses, linens, writing supplies, and bathroom items.
rationale: Task involves physically handling and placing tangible items (glasses, linens, supplies) in designated locations.

task: Prune or trim trees, shrubs, or hedges, using shears, pruners, or chain saws.
rationale: Requires physical operation of tools (shears, pruners, chain saws) and manual manipulation to cut vegetation.

task: Assemble prototypes or models of circuits, instruments, and systems for use in testing.
rationale: Assembling prototypes requires physical manipulation of components, soldering, wiring, and hands-on construction of circuits and instruments.

task: Repair, fabricate, and install steel or wood fittings, using blueprints, shop sketches, and instruction manuals.
rationale: Primary execution involves fabricating, installing, and physically manipulating steel/wood fittings—requires hands-on construction work and tool operation.
